name: Virgin de la Esperanza, Finca Cañaverosa, Arrozales – Ruta Circular – Yesares de Hellín
region_intro: Mountain biking around Calasparra offers diverse landscapes, characterized by mountainous terrain, river systems, and agricultural areas. The region features four rivers, including the prominent Segura River, and two reservoirs, creating varied environments for exploration. Riders can expect routes that traverse canyons, pass through rice fields, and navigate areas with significant elevation changes. This combination of natural features provides a range of mountain biking experiences. Best mountain bike trails around Calasparra The most popular mountain bike trail is Virgin de la Esperanza, Finca Cañaverosa, Arrozales – Ruta Circular – Yesares de Hellín, a difficult 31.8 miles (51.2 km) trail that takes 4 hours 20 minutes to complete, passing through varied agricultural and natural landscapes. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Río Segura and Cenajo Reservoir Loop, a moderate 26.9 miles (43.3 km) path. This route follows the Segura River and circles the Cenajo Reservoir, offering scenic views of the water and surrounding hills. Local mountain bikers also love the Calasparra – Salmerón loop from Calasparra, a 37.9 miles (61.0 km) trail leading through the local countryside, often completed in about 5 hours 2 minutes. Mountain biking around Calasparra is defined by river valleys, reservoirs, and mountainous terrain, with routes offering options for various ability levels from moderate to difficult. The routes in Calasparra are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 13 reviews. More than 121 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Calasparra's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Calasparra?
A: Calasparra offers a wide array of cycling routes, with over 120 cycle routes available to explore. Our guide features 6 mountain bike tours, ranging from moderate to difficult, providing diverse options for riders.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of mountain biking in Calasparra?
A: The mountain bike trails in Calasparra cater to various skill levels. Our selection includes 1 easy route, 2 moderate routes, and 3 difficult routes, ensuring options for both experienced riders and those looking for a challenge. The terrain often involves significant elevation changes and unpaved segments.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while mountain biking in Calasparra?
A: Mountain biking in Calasparra offers diverse landscapes, characterized by mountainous terrain, river systems, and agricultural areas. You'll encounter routes traversing canyons, passing through the famous Calasparra rice fields, and navigating areas with considerable elevation changes. The region is surrounded by four rivers, including the prominent Segura River, and two reservoirs, creating a rich and varied environment.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Calasparra?
A: Yes, many routes in Calasparra are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e929059060/virgin-de-la-esperanza-finca-canaverosa-arrozales-ruta-circular-yesares-de-hellin" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Virgin de la Esperanza, Finca Cañaverosa, Arrozales – Ruta Circular – Yesares de Hellín&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 31.8-mile (51.2 km) circular trail, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1187080889/rio-segura-and-cenajo-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Segura and Cenajo Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; offers a moderate 26.9-mile (43.3 km) ride.

Q: What are some notable natural attractions or landmarks I can explore along the trails?
A: Calasparra's trails often pass by significant natural and historical sites. You can explore the dramatic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5696563" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cañón de los Almacenes&lt;/a&gt;, a protected natural area, or ride near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1345858" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alfonso XIII Reservoir Dam and Quípar River Canyon&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2746743" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of the Virgin of Hope&lt;/a&gt; is another prominent landmark often visible from routes.

Q: When is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Calasparra?
A: Calasparra is particularly popular for cycling in May and December, offering pleasant temperatures for outdoor activities. The region's climate generally allows for mountain biking throughout much of the year, but spring and autumn provide ideal conditions with milder weather.

Q: Are there any routes that follow rivers or reservoirs?
A: Absolutely. Calasparra is defined by its river systems and reservoirs. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1187080889/rio-segura-and-cenajo-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Segura and Cenajo Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, offering scenic views as it follows the Segura River and circles the Cenajo Reservoir. You can also find routes near the confluence of the Moratalla and Segura Rivers.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Calasparra?
A: The mountain biking routes in Calasparra are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 13 reviews. More than 121 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore the region's varied terrain, often praising the diverse landscapes and challenging yet rewarding rides.

Q: Can I find routes that offer significant elevation gain for a challenging ride?
A: Yes, Calasparra's mountainous terrain provides ample opportunities for routes with significant elevation gain. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24525816" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calasparra – Salmerón loop from Calasparra&lt;/a&gt; features an ascent of over 950 meters, making it a demanding ride for those seeking a challenge.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through the famous Calasparra rice fields?
A: Yes, several routes offer views of or pass through the renowned Calasparra rice fields. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e929059060/virgin-de-la-esperanza-finca-canaverosa-arrozales-ruta-circular-yesares-de-hellin" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Virgin de la Esperanza, Finca Cañaverosa, Arrozales – Ruta Circular – Yesares de Hellín&lt;/a&gt; trail, for example, takes you through varied agricultural landscapes, including these iconic fields.

Q: Are there any routes suitable for a longer, multi-hour mountain biking adventure?
A: Absolutely. Calasparra offers several longer routes for extended rides. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17269725" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Arenal River Beach – Río Segura Riverside Area loop from Bosque de Cañaverosa&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 81.5 km (50.6 miles) trail that typically takes over 6 hours to complete, providing a full day's adventure.
tips: The Sanctuary is located in a place 6 km by road from the town. The hermitage houses two images of the Virgen de la Esperanza, patron saint of Calasparra, who are worshiped together. The primitive hermitage (XVII) was in one of the caves on the Segura river, a natural shelter for shepherds, to which new rooms and buildings have been added with the expansion of the cult. The creation of terraces up to the river has been succeeded in modern times by the creation of service areas: parking lots, restaurant, hotel, etc.
Looking closely at the water you can see where the cloudy water of the Moratalla River meets the clear water of the Segura River.

The Cañaverosa natural reserve is located in the upper reaches of the Segura River, in the regional northwest. It is 12 kilometers long and its protected surface is 225 hectares, between the municipalities of Moratalla and Calasparra. A place where you can hear the quiet flow of water and the chirping of birds

Calasparra rice is one of the only three rices with a protected designation of origin in Spain, along with those from the Ebro delta and Valencia. It is a mountain crop, at an approximate altitude of 450 meters. Irrigation is done through a system of terraces that allow constant renewal of water. It is harvested between October and November. Grain maturation takes 30% longer than other rice varieties.
A great place to rest for a moment and admire the water and natural beauty. Both the reservoir and the Camarillas dam are located in the Almadenes Canyon through which the Mundo River runs.
